findings.description: No candidate rules were extracted for this blob at all, despite it containing at least four distinct rule families: typography (Agenda font + email fallback), casing (ALL CAPS mandatory), color/tint treatment (40% tint on specific letters, two approved colorways), layout (three-line stack, left-aligned, hero-only), and fixed copy lockup. These should be split into separate clustered rules rather than left unclustered.

payload.rule_text: Campaign headline 'YOU DESERVE MORE' set in Agenda; digital may substitute Agenda as live text for this headline only; email fallback Nunito Sans ExtraBold.

findings.description: The 'ALL CAPS, always' statement is an absolute mandatory rule (governance: hard, always-applies) but no rule/governance facet was captured to record its hardness=hard and unconditional scope.

payload.rule_text: Headline must always be set in ALL CAPS.

findings.description: Tint specification (D and M letters at ~40% tint, tied symbolically to dermatomyositis) and the two approved colorways (Gold/Sunshine, Brand Blue) are compliance-relevant color rules but have no captured governance facets (hardness, approved-values enumeration, symbolic rationale).

payload.rule_text: Letters D (DESERVE) and M (MORE) set at ~40% tint of headline color; only two approved colorways: Gold/Sunshine or Brand Blue, tinted letters at 40% of same hue.

findings.description: The three-line stacking/left-alignment rule is scoped conditionally to 'hero placements' only; this scope restriction was not captured, risking over-application as a universal layout rule.

payload.rule_text: In hero placements only, stack headline as three lines (YOU / DESERVE / MORE), left-aligned.

findings.description: The full lockup copy line is a distinct fixed-copy/legal-style rule (exact required wording including brand name and disease reference) and should be its own rule rather than merged with typography/color rules, since it governs verbatim text not styling.

payload.rule_text: Full lockup copy must read exactly: 'YOU DESERVE MORE. So there's LISRAYA—the first targeted treatment for dermatomyositis.'

findings.description: The Agenda-font rule is scoped specifically to this headline only ('digital may use Agenda as live text for this headline only'); a missed relation is that this rule should be linked to the heading unit (u_...0000) establishing its scope is exclusively the 'YOU DESERVE MORE' campaign headline, not a general typography rule.
